Instead, she recommended laser removal first to give me the best possible result long-term. That level of honesty and expertise really stood out to me.

The laser treatment itself was quick and very manageable. I have one more session to go before moving forward with powder brows, and I already feel 100% confident that I’m in the best hands. Kashia’s artistry and attention to detail are evident, and I can’t wait to go back and continue the process.

Posting this in hopes of saving someone a few dollars and to help manage expectations.

I started coming to cleanslate last September and in my consult was told that my tattoos should be gone within 6-8 sessions, and for sure ahead of my wedding in December of this year.

I have had 10 sessions now with little to no change at $250 a session. Once we hit the 8 session mark, I asked if they would be gone by my wedding. I was told, no, but they have really good makeup to cover them up.

I would not have put myself through the pain or investment if I wanted to find “good makeup”

I understand each person is unique but I feel the expectations and progress were severely mismanaged.

After receiving another opinion and being told that tattoo removal is always a 2 year process, I have decided to stop going to cleanslate.

I know a lot of their positive reviews are from 2-5 years ago so not sure if something has changed with equipment but thought this experience would be helpful to anyone considering this process.

I wanted to get laser hair removal and this was one the places that I checked. Out of all the other places I checked, (including the most well-known national brand for laser hair removal) they were the most affordable and the most flexible. While other places tried to pressure me into signing long term contracts and loans, Skintellect offered me the ability to get one treatment or do a package bundle for a couple of treatments. I picked them for my laser hair removal, because they offered me double the treatments as the other places at less than a 3rd of the price. While other places were pushy and kept hounding me with lower prices, discounts and limited time sales that never materialized, Skintellect from the very beginning offered me a straight forward affordable price and excellent treatment. From my very first treatment I saw drastic results and as I am nearing the end of my treatment, I am so glad I was able to find them. In a very crowded marketplace, they stand out among their competitors due to their excellent service, remarkable treatments and affordable prices. Shoutout to Kristina, who has worked with me when I am either early or late for my appointments (I am driving from Orlando, so traffic is difficult to predict), and has always been extremely knowledgeable, friendly and professional.

My treatment was so quick, pain free and most importantly, they completely got rid of my tattoo. Their price is also very reasonable. I won’t go anywhere else but here if i need laser removal again

I am two weeks postop my FUE procedure with the folks at Enfuse and I couldn’t be happier with the evolving results & the process.

From the very beginning of my interest, Kevin was ready with any and all information, being very clear and realistic about what to expect for the procedure. He was very thoughtful and got ahead of many of my concerns, but did not sugarcoat or mislead me in any way about what to expect. Having gone through the procedure himself, he anticipated absolutely all of my needs & concerns before, during, and after the entire process.

FUE is a long-term investment and they all made it really clear what to expect over the next 12 months with the recovery time and long term follicle development. Dr Ioffe very clearly & concisely explained the process and assessed my health & medications while thoroughly reviewing my history.

During the procedure, Kevin and Dr. Ioffe worked gently & efficiently, keeping me informed of the progress while monitoring my well being. They & Patti were very conscious of asking if I needed breaks, how I was feeling, and assessing all of my needs.

For our lunch break, they ordered my favorite thing and set me up in a space privately, which was thoughtful & a nice break in a long day. They even made sure I was happy with whatever was playing on tv.

They prepared an aftercare kit that contained absolutely everything I needed for my recovery, many things I hadn’t considered. Again, it was so clear that Kevin had gone through this procedure himself and anticipated my every need. I followed their aftercare to the letter, and they’ve checked in often, confirming it has all gone according to plan.

I cannot stress enough, how safe and cared for I felt in there very professional, capable hands. I’m so grateful to have found these people.

Patti told me it was a strategic way to target layers of the skin and different types of damage complete rejuvenation. I said, sign me up and rejuvenate my skin! I must admit I was a little scared to do so much work & that my face might look crazy after. But the best thing is that each treatment works in a different depth and on a different skin concern. And when you combine them, you can get even results with less overall downtime! So I was so excited to do it, and she said that they would numb my face and it wouldn’t be that bad at all! The IPL evened the color and tone of my skin and made it brighter! Then the Frax helped with my fine lines and some of the mild scarring I have on my face. It also promotes collagen stimulation! I need all of that! The Morpheus8, which is all the rage now, truly has tightened my skin I’ve done at least four of these in the last few years! Patti explained to me that the Morpheus8 lifts from underneath where the regular lasers can’t get to! The Resolve helped smooth my skin tone & especially my dark spots and old acne scars that I have from when I was younger! Overall, it was the best thing I ever did! I was able to go to work the next day! There was some redness etc but nothing too crazy! My skin looks amazing. It looks like I have make up on and I have nothing on! Don’t be scared. It’s 100% worth it! Truly, my skin has never looked better and it was so worth it! This is the best preventative care you can do for yourself. It’s the best medicine you can do for yourself!

This company's technology looks amazing, and to its credit it may very well be a wonderful thing, HOWEVER, my personal experience with the clinic has convinced me that this is a very disorganized company, and furthermore one whose moral compass in terms of rational decision making is not skewed in a direction I admire.

After some initial phone tag, I was able to book an appointment for a first treatment with this clinic some weeks ago. However, because of the fact that I chose to be completely honest with my medical history at the time and disclosed that I believe I have a mild hypermobility syndrome (something I certainly could have kept secret if I’d known the trouble it would cause), my appointment was abruptly cancelled by the upper management due to (I would say extremely paranoid) concerns about liability issues. Please note, nothing on the website currently mentions that folks with a hypermobility disorder will be summarily rejected from treatment!!

Notably, I was not given any kind of say during this cancellation process. What irked me the most was I was not allowed to be part of any kind of conversation with management/the medical staff, despite the fact that this was a session that had been PROMISED to me and already BOOKED! And all this despite the fact that the tech who I had initially talked to said she was personally confident in and happy to give me the procedure despite the liability concerns, which she agreed were over the top given that I not only did not mind the idea of scarring, I actually thought it sounded cool were it to occur (I am a fan of the art form of scarification) and was willing to sign any kind of waiver to get the procedure done.

Finally, it was extremely puzzling and upsetting to me that my appointment was cancelled essentially behind my back and the only notification I got of the cancellation was an extremely vague text message saying “I spoke with our medical staff and just had a few things I wanted to touch base with you on.” Well, I had no idea what news was waiting in store for me, I figured the “few things” were just bureaucratic details, but I indeed tried calling back for several days in a row after this message. However, I was met with the answering machine each time (it seems like their phones are generally understaffed), and there was never any more personalized follow up from the clinic’s side. In other words, *I* had to chase THEM to finally get the confirmation that my appointment had indeed been cancelled since they didn’t have the courage(?)/common sense to tell me up front in the text they sent??? So, if I had not diligently followed up in this manner, I very well could have showed up to my appointment only to be told ON THE SPOT that my session had been cancelled without my knowledge or approval – and keep in mind, I am not living in Chicago full time right now so I was building my entire travel schedule around this one appointment.

Finally – and this is where it gets truly hilarious – weeks after the snafu with my cancelled appointment, I get a “follow up” text to an old message I had sent before scheduling the appointment saying “sorry this message got buried, when would you like to continue the conversation about booking?” I am no longer truly upset and can laugh about this now, but this company obviously needs to do some serious work in terms of client communications, PR, and staying on top of patient history.

Furthermore, it is unfortunately apparently so obsessed about liability issues that it can’t make a rational exception to take on a sane adult client in good faith (although I guess that’s the age we live in, thanks #america). It also seems to be totally insensitive to the psychological damage and upset it might be causing a prospective (and then later rejected) client by the snowball effect of such vague, contradictory, and misguided messages! Sorry, Tatt2Away, I wanted to love you - but come on, this is basic client rapport skills, 101!

This company's technology looks amazing, and to its credit it may very well be a wonderful thing, HOWEVER, my personal experience with the clinic has convinced me that this is a very disorganized company, and furthermore one whose moral compass in terms of rational decision making is not skewed in a direction I admire.

After some initial phone tag, I was able to book an appointment for a first treatment with this clinic some weeks ago. However, because of the fact that I chose to be completely honest with my medical history at the time and disclosed that I believe I have a mild hypermobility syndrome (something I certainly could have kept secret if I’d known the trouble it would cause), my appointment was abruptly cancelled by the upper management due to (I would say extremely paranoid) concerns about liability issues. Please note, nothing on the website currently mentions that folks with a hypermobility disorder will be summarily rejected from treatment!!

Notably, I was not given any kind of say during this cancellation process. What irked me the most was I was not allowed to be part of any kind of conversation with management/the medical staff, despite the fact that this was a session that had been PROMISED to me and already BOOKED! And all this despite the fact that the tech who I had initially talked to said she was personally confident in and happy to give me the procedure despite the liability concerns, which she agreed were over the top given that I not only did not mind the idea of scarring, I actually thought it sounded cool were it to occur (I am a fan of the art form of scarification) and was willing to sign any kind of waiver to get the procedure done.

Finally, it was extremely puzzling and upsetting to me that my appointment was cancelled essentially behind my back and the only notification I got of the cancellation was an extremely vague text message saying “I spoke with our medical staff and just had a few things I wanted to touch base with you on.” Well, I had no idea what news was waiting in store for me, I figured the “few things” were just bureaucratic details, but I indeed tried calling back for several days in a row after this message. However, I was met with the answering machine each time (it seems like their phones are generally understaffed), and there was never any more personalized follow up from the clinic’s side. In other words, *I* had to chase THEM to finally get the confirmation that my appointment had indeed been cancelled since they didn’t have the courage(?)/common sense to tell me up front in the text they sent??? So, if I had not diligently followed up in this manner, I very well could have showed up to my appointment only to be told ON THE SPOT that my session had been cancelled without my knowledge or approval – and keep in mind, I am not living in Chicago full time right now so I was building my entire travel schedule around this one appointment.

Finally – and this is where it gets truly hilarious – weeks after the snafu with my cancelled appointment, I get a “follow up” text to an old message I had sent before scheduling the appointment saying “sorry this message got buried, when would you like to continue the conversation about booking?” I am no longer truly upset and can laugh about this now, but this company obviously needs to do some serious work in terms of client communications, PR, and staying on top of patient history.

Furthermore, it is unfortunately apparently so obsessed about liability issues that it can’t make a rational exception to take on a sane adult client in good faith (although I guess that’s the age we live in, thanks #america). It also seems to be totally insensitive to the psychological damage and upset it might be causing a prospective (and then later rejected) client by the snowball effect of such vague, contradictory, and misguided messages! Sorry, Tatt2Away, I wanted to love you - but come on, this is basic client rapport skills, 101!

Keep in mind, the appointment was already confirmed in advance, and we couldn’t respond right away because we work night shifts and were asleep.

This policy (if you can even call it that) feels completely unreasonable, especially since we were paying customers and had never missed an appointment. Instead of showing any understanding or flexibility, the owner just cut us off. It’s disappointing and unprofessional.

If you’re considering this place, be prepared to walk on eggshells over arbitrary communication rules or risk losing your spot altogether. Definitely not worth the hassle in my opinion.

Edit: We never missed an appointment, we ended up having to move one, but you were never ghosted. You should just focus on your full-time job, and leave this type of business to people who can communicate in a more professional manner.

I’ve seen several different doctors/nurse injectors over the past couple years, and Clarity Skin takes the cake. One injector in specific, Emily, MUST HAVE SOLD HER SOUL TO THE DEVIL in exchange for the incredible ability she has. The injections she delivers are painless. In fact, I feel NOTHING. I seriously had no idea she had stuck a needle in me and when I asked about it, she said she was already done. I had to look in a mirror and see the injection site for myself to believe it. Perhaps her extensive experience being an OR nurse helped with that, as opposed to selling her soul to the devil.

Nah, it’s gotta be the devil thing.

P.S. the results are amazing. I waited a few months before reviewing just in case there were any surprises.

Edit: I went in again and my face got so messed up. I have TMJ and so Botox was recommended to weaken the masseter. At first, the TMJ pain subsided and I felt a noticeable difference. However, I soon *saw* a noticeable difference as well—and not a good one. The bottom half of my face got so thin and my jawline slackened. Should I have known Botox would have this effect? Yes, probably. However, I never even thought of it and was never told what the implications of receiving such treatment would entail, aesthetically speaking. I was embarrassed to go out into public. Also, I had happened to get a bad filler job around the same time, and for the first time, from them. I requested that filler be placed around and underneath my hollow eyes/dark circles, something I’ve had done many times before with amazing results. However, this time I came out looking like Maleficent and remained that way for months: high, rimmed orbital bones and pointy cheekbones, accompanied by a very slim face ending in a pointy chin.

Because I believe in good communication and in giving others a chance to try again, I went back. I told and showed them what was upsetting me and asked for help. It took several months to start building up my jaw muscles and then about two years to have it restored to normal. There was very little to be done except to wait out the Botox so the muscles could then rebuild themselves. They gave me a few hundred dollars off and generous samples of cosmetic and skin care products. I have waited years to edit this review so I could be further removed from this event and be as objective as possible in my feedback.
